papers.title: Data-driven and multi-mechanism strengthening based yield strength prediction in medium-manganese low-density steels
papers.paper_scope: Development and experimental validation of a data-driven yield strength prediction framework for medium-manganese low-density steels, integrating Gaussian Mixture Model data augmentation, Extra Trees ensemble learning, Genetic Algorithm hyperparameter optimization, and SHAP interpretability, verified on Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si steel annealed at 750, 800, and 850 degrees Celsius.

alloys.alloys_notes: Al and Si additions suppress carbide precipitation, resulting in negligible Orowan strengthening effect in this alloy system.

processes.description: Vacuum induction melting, hot forging, reheating at 1200 degrees C for 60 min, six-pass hot rolling from 1100 to 880 degrees C with 90% cumulative reduction, cold rolling with 60% total reduction, annealing at 750 degrees C for 30 min with air cooling, and tempering at 400 degrees C for 30 min with air cooling.
Identical processing route to proc_fe025c_750, but with annealing at 800 degrees C for 30 min with air cooling.
Identical processing route to proc_fe025c_750, but with annealing at 850 degrees C for 30 min with air cooling.

processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Steel melted in vacuum induction furnace.
Hot forging performed after melting.
Reheating before hot rolling.
Six-pass rolling on phi350 mm x 350 mm high-stiffness two-high hot rolling mill; initial rolling temperature 1100 degrees C, final rolling temperature 880 degrees C.
Cold rolling on four-high reversible cold rolling mill.
Intercritical annealing after cold rolling.
Tempering after annealing and air cooling.

structures.overall_structure: Dual-phase microstructure consisting of ferrite and bainite with minor retained austenite (less than 5%).
Dual-phase microstructure consisting of ferrite and bainite with minor retained austenite (less than 5%).

characterization_methods.technique: Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) with Image-Pro Plus image analysis
characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructural observation and statistical quantification of ferrite and bainite volume fractions and average grain sizes
characterization_methods.key_findings: Ferrite volume fraction 52% with average grain size 5.7 micrometers; bainite volume fraction 43.0% with average grain size 3.0 micrometers; retained austenite below 5%.

characterization_methods.technique: Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) with Image-Pro Plus image analysis
characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructural observation and statistical quantification of ferrite and bainite volume fractions and average grain sizes
characterization_methods.key_findings: Ferrite volume fraction 43% with average grain size 5.4 micrometers; bainite volume fraction 55.1% with average grain size 4.1 micrometers; retained austenite below 5%.

characterization_methods.technique: Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) with Image-Pro Plus image analysis
characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructural observation and statistical quantification of ferrite and bainite volume fractions and average grain sizes
characterization_methods.key_findings: Ferrite volume fraction 69% with average grain size 6.8 micrometers; bainite volume fraction 30.8% with average grain size 2.6 micrometers; retained austenite below 5%.

characterization_methods.purpose: Kernel Average Misorientation (KAM) analysis to determine Geometrically Necessary Dislocation (GND) density and Taylor factor
characterization_methods.key_findings: GND density 0.61×10^14 m^-2 and Taylor factor 3.45; EBSD scanning step size 0.2 micrometers.

characterization_methods.purpose: Kernel Average Misorientation (KAM) analysis to determine Geometrically Necessary Dislocation (GND) density and Taylor factor
characterization_methods.key_findings: GND density 0.84×10^14 m^-2 and Taylor factor 3.58; EBSD scanning step size 0.2 micrometers.

characterization_methods.key_findings: GND density 0.72×10^14 m^-2 and Taylor factor 3.53; EBSD scanning step size 0.2 micrometers.

characterization_methods.technique: Tensile testing (Instron 3382 universal electronic materials testing machine)
characterization_methods.purpose: Measure yield strength at room temperature
characterization_methods.key_findings: Yield strength 709 MPa at room temperature; gauge dimensions 15 mm x 5.0 mm x 1.0 mm; strain rate 5×10^-4 s^-1.

computational_details.simulation_method: Machine learning regression using Extremely Randomized Trees (Extra Trees) ensemble algorithm with Genetic Algorithm (GA) hyperparameter optimization and Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) data augmentation
computational_details.software_used: Python framework with DEAP 1.3.3 for GA optimization
computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Experimental verification on Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si steel yielded average prediction error 1.56%; validation against six literature datasets yielded average error 1.84%.

analytical_model.model_name: Classical linear superposition of strengthening mechanisms
analytical_model.equation_role: Baseline constitutive equation for yield strength feature extraction
analytical_model.model_note: Traditional physical metallurgy model (Eq. 1) used to quantify individual contributions of solid solution, grain refinement, dislocation, and Orowan strengthening as input features.

unmapped_findings: Original dataset of 177 entries was expanded to 776 using Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) data augmentation based on Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) and Expectation Maximization (EM) algorithm.
Extra Trees model test set performance before optimization: RMSE=76.2 MPa, MAPE=9.8%, R²=0.978. After GA optimization: RMSE=31.60 MPa, MAPE=4.39%, R²=0.997.
GA-optimized Extra Trees hyperparameters (Table 2): n_estimators=1657, max_depth=51, min_samples_split=3, min_samples_leaf=1.0, max_features=0.85, max_samples=0.92.
Comparison with alternative optimizations: Grid Search-ET RMSE=80.239 MPa, Bayesian-ET RMSE=80.721 MPa, versus GA-ET RMSE=31.60 MPa.

Prediction error reduction via GA optimization for experimental samples: 750 degrees C reduced by 22 MPa (Extra Trees diff 34 MPa to GA-ET diff 12 MPa); 800 degrees C reduced by 21 MPa (31 to 10 MPa); 850 degrees C reduced by 17 MPa (36 to 19 MPa).

Theoretical strengthening contributions and calculated vs actual YS (Table 3): AN750/ANT750 sigma0=220, sigmag=264, sigmad=263, sigmaOrowan=0 MPa, calculated YS=747 MPa; AN800/ANT800 sigma0=220, sigmag=256, sigmad=459, sigmaOrowan=0 MPa, calculated=935 MPa; AN850/ANT850 sigma0=220, sigmag=292, sigmad=414, sigmaOrowan=0 MPa, calculated=926 MPa.
Specimen designation inconsistency in text: Section 4.1 uses ANT750, ANT800, ANT850 while Section 4.2 and Table 3 use AN750, AN800, AN850, likely referring to the same specimen set.

Strengthening mechanism calculation formulas: solid solution delta_sigma0 = 32.3[Mn] + 83.2[Si] + 360[C] + 354.2[N] + 11[Mo] - 30[Cr] (Eq. 6); grain refinement sigma_g = K d^(-1/2) with K_alpha=400 MPa·micrometer^(1/2) and K_bainite=700 MPa·micrometer^(1/2) (Eq. 7); dislocation sigma_d = alpha M G b rho^(1/2) with alpha=0.4, M=Taylor factor, G=81 GPa, b=0.25 nm (Eq. 9); GND density rho_GND = 2 theta / (mu b) with mu=0.2 micrometer (Eq. 10).

Fig. 12 engineering stress-strain curve data points from figure table: approximate stress values at engineering strains 5-35% are provided for 750, 800, and 850 degrees C annealed samples, showing peak flow stresses around 1050-1300 MPa depending on annealing temperature.

SHAP interpretability analysis (Fig. 17) feature importance ranking: dislocation strengthening (0.48), Orowan strengthening (0.27), fine grain strengthening (0.19), solid solution strengthening (0.05); dislocation strengthening is identified as the dominant YS contributor.

Literature validation dataset (Table 5) includes two compositions: Fe-0.29C-12.14Mn-2.96Al-0.11V-0.063Nb (three processing conditions) and Fe-9.7Mn-2.04Al-0.99Si-0.52V-0.42C (three processing conditions), with GA-Extra Trees predictions yielding average error 1.84% compared to measured YS values ranging from 591.5 to 1665.4 MPa.
